mentions.note: Held for the Charitable Trust; CEO Charlie Sharf has pivoted the bank toward higher-margin corporate/M&A business with strong cost control.
Miss driven mainly by severance costs, not core business weakness; trimmed for the Charitable Trust but the stock likely still has further to fall before it bottoms.
Michelle asks which businesses benefit from AI beyond chips/energy/infra. Banks will benefit most from AI efficiency; Wells Fargo has best handle on it — we own for Charitable Trust.
Winner as software buyer, not maker. Hired AWS technologist to find hidden software spending savings. Software users benefiting from AI-driven efficiency.
Cramer's favorite bank; doing best job of integrating AI with recent AWS hire; positioned to cut costs effectively.
Owned in travel trust; long-term turnaround story under CEO Charlie Sharpe who's willing to buy back stock aggressively; not an earnings story but fundamental transformation play.
Surprised by large private credit exposure; worst of major banks on that front. Missed a quarter but stock trying to bottom; Cramer owns it for trust on valuation despite disappointment.
Despite soft quarter, +11.7% earnings growth at 11.5× earnings; private equity exposure mostly benign; cheapest bank on valuation; turnaround story.
Trading at 11x earnings, among cheapest stocks in market; similar valuation to BAC; value appearance but drivers unclear.
Bank rally beneficiary on rate environment, strong consumer, deregulation; cheap valuation leaves room for 50%+ rally before reasonably priced.
Quarter terrific but savaged by analysts obsessed with NIM/NII; CEO Scharf transforming into merchant bank winning in M&A, IPOs; trades 12x earnings — told Investing Club it's a steal.
